operating_state: Total revenue reached $23.35 billion in Q3 2023, representing 9% growth driven by automotive sales increasing from higher Model 3/Y deliveries despite lower average selling prices from price reductions and unfavorable foreign exchange. Energy generation and storage revenue surged 40% primarily from increased Megapack deployments. Services revenue grew 32% from higher used vehicle sales, Supercharging revenue, and maintenance services reflecting the expanding vehicle fleet.
cost_structure: Total automotive gross margin compressed from 27.9% to 18.7% primarily due to lower average selling prices partially offset by reduced per-unit costs from improved manufacturing efficiency, lower material costs, and IRA manufacturing credits. Energy storage gross margin expanded dramatically to 24.4% driven by lower Megapack production costs and higher-margin product mix. Operating expenses increased with R&D rising for Cybertruck pre-production and AI programs while SG&A grew from higher headcount and facilities costs.
material_events: Vaibhav Taneja was appointed Chief Financial Officer effective August 4, 2023, succeeding Zachary Kirkhorn. Tesla entered into a derivative lawsuit settlement agreement in July 2023 subject to Delaware Court of Chancery approval with final hearing scheduled for October 13, 2023. The company announced a new Megafactory in Shanghai to support energy storage production expansion.
macro_exposures: Rising interest rates from central bank actions have impacted vehicle lease and finance affordability, affecting order rates and pricing. Inflationary pressures across operating markets have increased costs while strengthening US dollar created negative foreign exchange impacts on revenue. Industry-wide component constraints, labor shortages, and supplier liquidity challenges pose ongoing supply chain risks affecting component access and costs.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to exceed $9.00 billion in 2023 and range between $7.00-$9.00 billion in each of the following two fiscal years to support global factory expansion and new product development. The company is focused on ramping Cybertruck production, expanding Supercharger network to accommodate NACS adoption by other manufacturers, and increasing energy storage production capacity while maintaining self-funding capability through continued vertical integration and cost reduction initiatives.
uncertainty_profile: Production ramp execution remains subject to manufacturing technology risks and component supply constraints. Cybertruck and next-generation platform development face ambitious technological targets with uncertain timing. Energy storage deployment timing varies significantly quarter-to-quarter based on project milestones. The pending derivative lawsuit settlement requires court approval with potential for modification or rejection.
